strengths: 20 years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform with real-world adoption by Green Map
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and community leadership via conference talks (FOSS4G 2019) and multi-day workshops and courses
concerns: No recognized AI expertise or demonstrated AI-powered products or platforms; AI exposure is limited to recent MCP code-generator and agent-tooling contributions since 2025
Career ceiling is Senior/Staff engineer; no evidence of Director-level scope, organization-wide technical strategy, or advising strategic customers and partners
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and cloud experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) is applied rather than platform-strategy depth

key_factors.factor: AI expertise and AI product track record

response.justification: The role's core competencies are recognized AI expertise with shipped AI products, cloud platform strategy, Director-level influence, and 15+ years of engineering. The candidate clearly meets the tenure bar (professional work since 2006, including staff-level roles) and the entrepreneurial preference (founded GISCollective, used in production by Green Map). However, the central competency — recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI-built products — is essentially absent: the only cited evidence is contributing to MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq since April 2025, which is early-stage involvement rather than recognized leadership. There is also no evidence of operating at Director level, setting product strategy, or advising strategic customers; his talks and workshops show community teaching rather than industry-level influence. Strong senior generalist, but a poor evidence-based fit for a principal AI-strategy position.
